interaction_flow: StreamlitUI → init(): @st.cache_resource — runs once per session
init() → threading.Thread(target=agent.run, daemon=True).start(): agent loop running in background
StreamlitUI → init_session_state(): set defaults (streaming=False, display_queue=None, partial_response='', messages=[])
StreamlitUI → render_message(role, content, ts) [stapp2] / with st.chat_message + render_segments() [stapp]: display each message
GeneraticAgent (daemon) → display_queue.put({'next': partial_text}): on each partial token/chunk
agent_backend_stream → StreamlitUI: yield item['next'] (partial) or yield item['done'] (final, break)
StreamlitUI → fold_turns(response): parse 'LLM Running (Turn N)...' markers into segments list
StreamlitUI → render_segments([segs[-1]], suffix=' ▌'): render live (last) segment with cursor
StreamlitUI → render_segments([segs[frozen]]); live = st.empty(); frozen++: promote completed segments to frozen slots
StreamlitUI (on 'done') → render_segments(remaining_segs): finalize all segments without cursor
start_agent_task → st.session_state: display_queue=queue, streaming=True, stopping=False, partial_response='', reply_ts=now
render_streaming_area → render_message('assistant', seg + '▌', ts=reply_ts): display partial with cursor
poll_agent_output → display_queue.get_nowait(): drain up to 20 items
poll_agent_output (if done) → st.session_state: streaming=False, stopping=False, display_queue=None
render_streaming_area → st.rerun(): loop back until done
key_relationships: GeneraticAgent.run() executes in background daemon thread (started once)
StreamlitUI communicates with agent exclusively via display_queue (queue.Queue)
display_queue carries {'next': str} for partial updates and {'done': str} for completion
stapp uses Python generator (for response in agent_backend_stream) for streaming
stapp2 uses Streamlit rerun loop (st.rerun + session_state polling) for streaming
Both apps use fold_turns / _get_response_segments to parse multi-turn LLM output

state_machine_stapp2.streaming: streaming=True, display_queue=Queue, partial_response updates each rerun
state_machine_stapp2.stopping: stopping=True (abort sent), waits for done signal

key_methods.init(): @st.cache_resource; creates GeneraticAgent; starts agent.run() daemon; returns agent
key_methods.agent_backend_stream(prompt) [stapp]: generator; polls display_queue; yields partial/final text; calls agent.abort() in finally
key_methods.start_agent_task(prompt) [stapp2]: calls agent.put_task; stores queue in session_state; sets streaming=True
key_methods.poll_agent_output(max_items) [stapp2]: drains queue non-blocking; updates partial_response; returns done:bool
key_methods.render_streaming_area() [stapp2]: renders live partial; polls queue; calls finish_streaming_message on done; st.rerun()
key_methods.finish_streaming_message() [stapp2]: appends final segments to messages; resets streaming state
key_methods.fold_turns(text) [stapp]: splits text on 'LLM Running (Turn N)...' → list of {type:text|fold, content} segments
key_methods._get_response_segments(text) [stapp2]: splits text on turn markers; returns list of segment strings
key_methods.render_segments(segments, suffix) [stapp: renders each segment as st.expander or st.markdown

rendering_pattern.stapp: st.empty() slots + slot.container() + render_segments(); frozen slots for completed turns, live slot for last segment with cursor '▌'
rendering_pattern.stapp2: st.empty().container() wraps all current segments; cursor appended to last segment; entire area re-rendered each rerun

important_notes: Both stapp and stapp2 import GeneraticAgent from agentmain
agent.run() is a long-running background loop; frontend communicates via queue only
display_queue items: {'next': str} = partial update, {'done': str} = final response
stapp uses Python generator for streaming (blocking for-loop with yield); stapp2 uses non-blocking polling with st.rerun()
agent.abort() is called in stapp finally block or via 'stop' button in stapp2
Multi-turn responses are visually folded: earlier turns → st.expander, last turn → visible
stapp2 has richer session state tracking: reply_ts, current_prompt, stopping flag, autonomous_enabled
